figures.caption: Fig. 2. PER sweep under the GE packet-erasure channel at K = 8, with absolute AoIS-AUC curves (top) and paired gaps to the two block-based baselines (bottom). Hollow and filled markers denote bc = 2 and bc = 3, respectively.
Fig. 3. PER sweep of AoIS-AUC at K = 16.
Fig. 4. Protocol audit under PER sweeps at bc = 2: package-transport ratio and average package span for K ∈ {4, 8, 16}.
Fig. 5. Recovery-time gap under PER sweeps for K ∈ {8, 16} and bc ∈ {2, 3}. More negative values indicate earlier threshold crossing.
Fig. 6. Motion-stratified AoIS-AUC and recovery-time gaps under PER sweeps.

Fig. 1. System overview of receiver-driven semantic HARQ for generative video reconstruction. The receiver requests tube-structured packages, the forward link transports them as atomic HARQ objects, and successful deliveries are committed before diffusion inpainting and the next feedback decision.
